title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Valerie Hobhouse provides Inspector Sharp with an account of an evening at the dormitory, noting that most of the boys had gone out, while Celia Austin and Gene Thompson went to bed early, leaving Valerie and Sally Finch up late. 

Sharp then interrogates Patricia Lane regarding the destruction of Elizabeth Johnston's paper. Patricia defends Nigel Chapman against suspicion, asserting that Celia Austin was actually responsible for the damage. She describes Nigel as a shy, moody individual whose sarcasm is a product of an unhappy childhood and a strained relationship with his strict father following his mother's death. Sharp observes that Patricia appears to be in love with Nigel and considers whether her affection provides a motive for murder, though he notes that the engagement between Sylvia Austin and Colin McNaughton might rule out such motives.

Finally, Sharp interviews Gene Thompson regarding the death of Sylvia Austin. Sharp suggests that the poison used may have been obtained from St. Catherine's Hospital, where Gene visits the pharmacy to see Millicent Keller. Gene denies any involvement with the poison. Regarding the thefts, Gene maintains that Sylvia's actions were a simple lack of honesty rather than a psychological compulsion. Gene expresses a negative view of Colin McNaughton, labeling him an atheist and a communist, and suggests McNaughton supported Celia because of a lack of respect for property. Gene also claims that a man named Poirot was the one who discovered Sylvia's actions, a claim Sharp disputes, noting that Poirot only suggested calling the police.
